entities.summary: Enumeration of all valid lifecycle states for a generation request

detail.name: SequenceStatus
detail.description: WAITING (queued), RUNNING (active on GPU), FINISHED_STOPPED, FINISHED_LENGTH, FINISHED_ABORT. Explicit states prevent invalid operations.
detail.why: Without explicit states, you might accidentally free blocks of a running sequence or schedule a finished one

takeaway.explain: Use an enum to define all valid states. Only declared transitions are allowed. Compiler/IDE catches illegal values.
takeaway.rationale: Boolean flags (is_running, is_finished) allow impossible combinations like running=true AND finished=true. Enum makes these impossible by construction.
takeaway.cross_lang: Rust enum (can carry data per variant), TypeScript union types, Java enum, Swift enum with associated values
takeaway.gotcha: Adding a new state later requires updating every switch/match — use exhaustive matching to catch missing cases at compile time

entities.summary: Full lifecycle of one generation request: prompt tokens through generation to finish

detail.name: Sequence
detail.description: Tracks all per-request state: token IDs, sampling params, logical block assignments, and status transitions. Persists across scheduling steps.
detail.why: Each request has unique mutable state (tokens generated so far, KV cache blocks used) that must survive across many scheduling steps

takeaway.explain: Object with well-defined states and transitions. Only certain state changes are allowed. Invalid transitions raise errors.
takeaway.rationale: A Sequence moves WAITING→RUNNING→FINISHED. Without SM discipline, a bug could skip RUNNING and go straight to FINISHED, leaking allocated blocks.
takeaway.cross_lang: Rust typestate pattern (different type per state), Java State pattern (GoF), TypeScript discriminated unions

takeaway.explain: Indirection layer: logical addresses map to physical locations via a table. Enables moving data without updating all references.
takeaway.rationale: Sequences use logical block indices. Physical GPU blocks can be rearranged without invalidating sequence state.
takeaway.cross_lang: OS virtual memory (page tables), database logical/physical record IDs

detail.design: Separates token management from block management within same object — locality without coupling
detail.convention: Sequence is read-only outside engine internals

entities.summary: Initialize sequence with prompt tokens and sampling configuration

detail.name: __init__
detail.description: Creates new sequence: assigns unique ID, stores prompt tokens, sets initial WAITING status, allocates first logical block.
detail.why: Every generation starts here — establishes baseline state that scheduler and block manager will operate on

takeaway.explain: Constructor creates object in simplest valid state. No multi-step initialization needed. Object is usable immediately after __init__.
takeaway.rationale: If __init__ left the object half-initialized, callers would need to remember to call .setup() — fragile and error-prone.
takeaway.cross_lang: Rust: all fields must be set in struct literal (compiler enforces). Java: constructor establishes invariants.
takeaway.gotcha: Dont do heavy I/O or allocation in __init__ unless you want slow object creation. Consider factory methods for expensive setup.

entities.summary: Return total number of tokens (prompt + generated)

detail.name: __len__
detail.description: Enables len(sequence) syntax. Returns prompt length + completion length.

takeaway.explain: Implement __len__ to make class work with len(). Part of Python sized protocol. Enables intuitive API for collection-like objects.

detail.name: __getitem__
detail.description: Enables sequence[i] syntax for token access.

takeaway.explain: Implement __getitem__ for indexing syntax obj[i]. Part of sequence protocol. Can also enable iteration and slicing.
takeaway.rationale: Enables sequence[i] syntax for token access

entities.summary: Predicate checking if generation has terminated

detail.name: is_finished
detail.description: Returns True if status is any FINISHED_ variant. Used by scheduler to know when to free resources.

takeaway.explain: Method returning bool that encapsulates a business-logic check. Centralizes condition rather than repeating enum checks.
takeaway.rationale: Returns True if status is any FINISHED_ variant

entities.summary: Property returning count of generated tokens so far

detail.name: num_completion_tokens
detail.description: Computed property: total length minus prompt length.

takeaway.explain: @property makes method accessible as attribute. Computed on access, not stored. Keeps derived values always consistent with source.
takeaway.rationale: Computed property: total length minus prompt length
takeaway.cross_lang: C# properties (get/set), Kotlin var with custom getter, Ruby attr_accessor, Java getter/setter
takeaway.gotcha: Heavy computation in a property looks like attribute access — callers wont expect it to be slow

takeaway.explain: List slicing in Python creates a copy. For large sequences, consider memoryview or numpy views to avoid copying.
list[start:end] extracts sub-sequence. Omit start for 0, omit end for len. Negative indices count from end.

entities.summary: Compute number of KV cache blocks used by this sequence

detail.name: num_blocks
detail.description: Ceiling division of total tokens by block size.

takeaway.explain: -(-a // b) or math.ceil(a/b). Computes how many fixed-size blocks needed to hold variable-size content.
takeaway.rationale: Ceiling division of total tokens by block size

entities.summary: Count tokens in the last (potentially partial) block

detail.name: last_block_num_tokens
detail.description: total_tokens % block_size, or block_size if exactly full.

takeaway.explain: n % block_size gives fill level of last block. If 0, block is full (use block_size). Common in paged/chunked systems.
takeaway.rationale: total_tokens % block_size, or block_size if exactly full

entities.summary: Get logical block index for a given token position

detail.name: block
detail.description: Maps token position to its containing block: position // block_size.

takeaway.explain: block_index = position // block_size. Fundamental operation in any paged/blocked memory system. O(1) mapping.
takeaway.rationale: Maps token position to its containing block: position // block_size

entities.summary: Extends sequence by one generated token, managing block boundaries

detail.name: append_token
detail.description: Appends new token to completion, allocates new logical block if current is full.
detail.why: Token-by-token autoregressive generation — each step adds exactly one token

takeaway.explain: Model generates one token per step, using all previous tokens as context. Append loop continues until stop condition.
takeaway.rationale: Used here because: Token-by-token autoregressive generation — each step adds exactly one token

detail.perf: Called once per token per sequence per step — must be O(1)

entities.summary: Custom pickle serialization excluding non-serializable fields

detail.name: __getstate__
detail.description: Controls what gets serialized when sequence is pickled (for preemption/migration).

takeaway.explain: __getstate__ controls serialization. Return dict of what to save. Exclude file handles, locks, GPU tensors that cant be pickled.
takeaway.rationale: Controls what gets serialized when sequence is pickled (for preemption/migration)

entities.summary: Custom pickle deserialization restoring sequence state

detail.name: __setstate__
detail.description: Restores sequence from pickled state, re-initializes transient fields.

takeaway.explain: Counterpart to __getstate__. Receives saved dict, must reconstruct full object. Re-initialize transient resources (connections, caches).
takeaway.rationale: Restores sequence from pickled state, re-initializes transient fields
